title: America in the 1920s
description: Test your knowledge of the changes that American culture and society underwent in the 1920s.

assessment_items.question_content:
What was one strategy of the **National American Woman Suffrage Association (NAWSA)** in its campaign to secure voting rights for women? 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Fifteenth Amendment was passed in 1870, fifty years before women got the right to vote in 1920.

choices.content: Focusing on changing state laws instead of federal law
Opposing World War I to garner the support of pacifists
Supporting the Fifteenth Amendment, which granted suffrage to African American men, with the hope that it would soon lead to woman suffrage

assessment_items.question_content:
**President Hoover** has a reputation for dithering in the face of the Great Depression, but what are some policies he enacted to counteract the economic crisis?


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The US Foreign Assistance Act was not created until 1961 and focused on assisting developing nations.

choices.content: He launched a public works program, helped to pass the Glass-Steagall Act of 1932, and increased labor protections and agriculture subsidies, but he also signed the ill-advised Smoot-Hawley Tariff Act.
President Hoover implemented the New Deal, including the Social Security Act of 1935, which provided insurance to disabled and elderly citizens.
To counteract the economic crisis, President Hoover passed the US Foreign Assistance Act, which declared that the United States would divert funds to assist other nations with their financial crises.

assessment_items.question_content:
What fears motivated Congress to pass the **Immigration Act of 1924**, which restricted the inflow of Southern and Eastern European immigrants?




[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: France is in **western** Europe, and there was never a governor of New York named Wallace T. Sneed.

choices.content: Labor unrest, the Bolshevik Revolution, and rising racist, nativist sentiment had made Congress wary of allowing poor Europeans who had been exposed to socialist ideas to immigrate
Anglo-Americans worried that they’d lose their culinary traditions if too many Italians and Russians immigrated to the United States.
The proudly Francophobic governor of New York, Wallace T. Sneed, petitioned Congress to prevent French people from immigrating to the United States.

assessment_items.question_content:
Ratified in 1919, what did the **Eighteenth Amendment** to the United States Constitution establish?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: An age limit was never placed on the bill.

choices.content: It made illegal “the manufacture, sale, and transportation of intoxicating liquors” into and within the United States.
It declared that only citizens who were 18 years or older could manufacture, sell, and transport liquors.
It banned women from purchasing and consuming liquor, on the basis that it would have harmful effects on their health.

assessment_items.question_content:
If the Eighteenth Amendment made the sale, manufacture, and transportation of alcohol illegal, what was the **Volstead Act** for?




[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: It took another Constitutional Amendment, not just an Act, to repeal Prohibition.

choices.content: The Eighteenth Amendment empowered Congress to enforce the Amendment through legislation; the Volstead Act enumerated the ways in which Prohibition would actually be carried out.
The Volstead Act protected select businesses who bought a liquor license before 1919, to manufacture, sell and transport liquor.
The Volstead Act was the law that repealed the Eighteenth Amendment, making alcohol legal to produce, sell, and transport once again.

assessment_items.question_content:
The **Lost Generation** was a group of writers, artists, musicians, and intellectuals who

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: None of the Lost Generation artists were aboard the _Lusitania_.

choices.content: lived as expatriates in Paris and expressed their cynicism about American life and culture, having been disillusioned by the carnage of the First World War.
died aboard the _Lusitania_ in 1915; they were en route to Ireland for an artistic conference.
vanished from the art world for thirty years; following the chaos of the war, they were too demoralized to create literature, art, or music.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Zora Neale Hurston** and **Langston Hughes** were two of the many luminaries that flourished in the


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Hughes spent time in Paris, but wasn’t part of the expatriate community there.

choices.content: Harlem Renaissance in Upper Manhattan.
Lost Generation community of American expatriates in Montmartre, Paris.
Delta Empire, an African-American arts community formed around the University of Mississippi in Oxford, MS.

assessment_items.question_content:
The **Nineteenth Amendment** to the United States Constitution guaranteed


[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: It was the _Eighteenth_ Amendment that enacted Prohibition.

choices.content: that the right to vote shall not be abridged on account of a citizen’s sex.
that Congress shall make no law abridging the freedom of speech or religion.
that the sale, manufacture, and transport of intoxicating beverages was illegal within the United States and its territories.
